response.justification: Bogdan Szabo has a broad 15+ year software career, but his experience is overwhelmingly in web technologies — TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang — rather than C++. His C++ exposure is limited to a 6-month role at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, which falls far short of the deep C++/STL expertise this role demands as its core competency. There is no evidence of MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850), secure development lifecycle practices per IEC 62443, or any industrial automation domain background. The high-throughput distributed systems work at Sociomantic is genuinely relevant, and Node.js/React exposure matches the advantageous skills list, but these do not compensate for the critical gaps in the primary technical requirements.
strengths: Demonstrated high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP req/s at Sociomantic using async I/O and concurrent patterns)
Event-driven architecture background from the GISCollective open-source platform aligns with message-broker design patterns
Node.js and React exposure directly matches the role's listed advantageous skills
concerns: C++ proficiency is superficial — only ~6 months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a thesis project; no evidence of production STL usage, multi-threading, or high-performance C++ systems at scale
No industrial automation, IoT, or relevant protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0) anywhere in the resume
No mention of secure development lifecycle, IEC 62443 standards, or application security practices despite SDL being a core responsibility
